rulings.Case Title: THOMAS RUBINO et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, AUGUST 8, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1. Defendant NMBFil, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to negligence, strict liability and survival and granted as to punitive damages and off calendar as to false representation and enterprise liability. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiffs submitted evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Rubino was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (Bondo auto body filler) attributable to defendant. As to punitive damages, defendant sustained its burden and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiffs represent that they have dismissed their causes of action for false representation and enterprise liability. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND BARRAGAN VS. THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING COMPANY
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, August 8, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Intervenors St. Paul Fire and Marine Insurance Co., et al¿s joinder in Scott Co. of California¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied. Intervenors¿ failed to provide proper notice of their joinder pursuant to C.C.P. section 437c(a). Further, Intervenors attempted to join a motion that was improperly filed by a suspended corporation. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND BARRAGAN VS. THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING COMPANY
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, August 8, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Scott Co. of California¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Scott Co. was a suspended corporation when it filed and served its motion and did not have the ability to defend itself. Defendant¿s filing of such motion was improper. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
